title: Belgium Schengen Tourist / Short-Stay Visa
snippet: Belgium is a full member of the Schengen Area. A single Type C visa lets you stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period across all Schengen countries, for tourism, family visits or business.
keywords: Belgium is a full member of the Schengen Area. A single Type C visa lets you stay up to 90 days within any 180-day period across all Schengen countries, for tourism, family visits or business.
Typically 15 calendar days from a complete application; can extend up to 30-60 days during peak season or if additional checks are required.
Up to 90 days within any 180-day period, multiple-entry depending on issuance.

title: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Belgium?
snippet: Yes. Indian passport holders need a Schengen Type C visa for tourism or short business visits of up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Apply through VFS Global.
keywords: Do Indian citizens need a visa to visit Belgium?
Yes. Indian passport holders need a Schengen Type C visa for tourism or short business visits of up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Apply through VFS Global.

title: How long does a Belgium visa take to process?
snippet: Typically around 15 calendar days for a complete application, but it can take up to 30-60 days during busy periods, so apply as early as possible (up to 6 months ahead, and no later than 15 days before travel).
keywords: How long does a Belgium visa take to process?

Typically around 15 calendar days for a complete application, but it can take up to 30-60 days during busy periods, so apply as early as possible (up to 6 months ahead, and no later than 15 days before travel).

title: What is the emergency number in Belgium?
snippet: Dial 112 for any emergency (ambulance, fire or police) anywhere in Belgium — it's toll-free. You can also dial 101 directly for police or 100 for ambulance/fire.
keywords: What is the emergency number in Belgium?
Dial 112 for any emergency (ambulance, fire or police) anywhere in Belgium — it's toll-free. You can also dial 101 directly for police or 100 for ambulance/fire.

title: Where is the Indian Embassy in Belgium located?
snippet: The Embassy of India is at 217 Chaussée de Vleurgat, 1050 Brussels. It covers Belgium, Luxembourg and the EU institutions.
keywords: Where is the Indian Embassy in Belgium located?
The Embassy of India is at 217 Chaussée de Vleurgat, 1050 Brussels. It covers Belgium, Luxembourg and the EU institutions.

title: How do I get from Brussels Airport into the city?
snippet: The Brussels Airport Express train takes about 18 minutes to Brussels-Central (~€8.90). The STIB Airport Bus (Line 12) is a cheaper alternative into the EU Quarter (~€7.90 with a MOBIB or contactless card).
keywords: How do I get from Brussels Airport into the city?

The Brussels Airport Express train takes about 18 minutes to Brussels-Central (~€8.90). The STIB Airport Bus (Line 12) is a cheaper alternative into the EU Quarter (~€7.90 with a MOBIB or contactless card).

title: What currency is used in Belgium?
snippet: The Euro (EUR). Contactless card payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are common in all cities.
keywords: What currency is used in Belgium?
The Euro (EUR). Contactless card payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are common in all cities.

title: Is Belgium safe for solo travellers?
snippet: Belgium is generally very safe. As in any European city, be alert for pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas and on public transport.
keywords: Is Belgium safe for solo travellers?
Belgium is generally very safe. As in any European city, be alert for pickpocketing in crowded tourist areas and on public transport.

title: Can I find vegetarian and Jain food in Belgium?
snippet: Yes, especially in Brussels and Antwerp (particularly around the Diamond District), which have dedicated Indian vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants.
keywords: Can I find vegetarian and Jain food in Belgium?
Yes, especially in Brussels and Antwerp (particularly around the Diamond District), which have dedicated Indian vegetarian and Jain-friendly restaurants.

title: What languages are spoken in Belgium?
snippet: Belgium has three official languages: Dutch (Flanders), French (Wallonia) and German (small eastern region). Brussels is officially bilingual French/Dutch. English is widely understood in cities.
keywords: What languages are spoken in Belgium?
Belgium has three official languages: Dutch (Flanders), French (Wallonia) and German (small eastern region). Brussels is officially bilingual French/Dutch. English is widely understood in cities.

title: Do I need to register with the local municipality after arriving?
snippet: Yes, if staying longer than 90 days, non-EU nationals must register their address at the local commune/gemeente (municipality) within 8 working days of arrival.
keywords: Do I need to register with the local municipality after arriving?
Yes, if staying longer than 90 days, non-EU nationals must register their address at the local commune/gemeente (municipality) within 8 working days of arrival.

title: How do public transport tickets work across Belgium?
snippet: Each region has its own operator — STIB (Brussels), De Lijn (Flanders), TEC (Wallonia) and SNCB (national trains). MOBIB cards and contactless bank cards work on most local networks; SNCB tickets are separate.
keywords: How do public transport tickets work across Belgium?

Each region has its own operator — STIB (Brussels), De Lijn (Flanders), TEC (Wallonia) and SNCB (national trains). MOBIB cards and contactless bank cards work on most local networks; SNCB tickets are separate.

title: What should I pack for Belgium's weather?
snippet: Belgium has a mild, rainy maritime climate. Pack layers, a waterproof jacket, and comfortable walking shoes year-round; winters are cold and grey, summers are mild.
keywords: What should I pack for Belgium's weather?
Belgium has a mild, rainy maritime climate. Pack layers, a waterproof jacket, and comfortable walking shoes year-round; winters are cold and grey, summers are mild.

title: Can I use my Indian driving licence in Belgium?
snippet: An Indian driving licence with an International Driving Permit (IDP) can be used for short stays (up to 185 days). For residents, conversion to a Belgian licence is required within that window.
keywords: Can I use my Indian driving licence in Belgium?
An Indian driving licence with an International Driving Permit (IDP) can be used for short stays (up to 185 days). For residents, conversion to a Belgian licence is required within that window.

title: Are there Hindu temples or gurudwaras in Belgium?
snippet: Yes — Brussels and Antwerp have Hindu temples and gurudwaras serving the Indian community. See the Indian Community section for details.
keywords: Are there Hindu temples or gurudwaras in Belgium?
Yes — Brussels and Antwerp have Hindu temples and gurudwaras serving the Indian community. See the Indian Community section for details.

title: How do I renew my Indian passport while in Belgium?
snippet: Passport renewal is handled by the Embassy of India in Brussels. Check indianembassybrussels.gov.in for the latest application forms and appointment system.
keywords: How do I renew my Indian passport while in Belgium?
Passport renewal is handled by the Embassy of India in Brussels. Check indianembassybrussels.gov.in for the latest application forms and appointment system.

title: Is healthcare good in Belgium?
snippet: Belgium has a high-quality healthcare system. Visitors should carry travel insurance; residents must enrol in mandatory health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds).
keywords: Belgium has a high-quality healthcare system. Visitors should carry travel insurance; residents must enrol in mandatory health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds).

title: Can I work in Belgium on a tourist visa?
snippet: No. A Schengen tourist visa does not permit employment. You need a work permit or single permit tied to a specific job offer.
keywords: Can I work in Belgium on a tourist visa?
No. A Schengen tourist visa does not permit employment. You need a work permit or single permit tied to a specific job offer.

title: What is the best way to travel between Belgian cities?
snippet: SNCB trains are fast, frequent and connect all major cities — usually the best option over driving, especially between Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent and Bruges.
keywords: What is the best way to travel between Belgian cities?
SNCB trains are fast, frequent and connect all major cities — usually the best option over driving, especially between Brussels, Antwerp, Ghent and Bruges.

title: Do I need an adapter for electrical outlets in Belgium?
snippet: Belgium uses the Type E plug (230V, 50Hz), the same as most of continental Europe. Indian plugs (Type C/D) will need an adapter.
keywords: Do I need an adapter for electrical outlets in Belgium?
Belgium uses the Type E plug (230V, 50Hz), the same as most of continental Europe. Indian plugs (Type C/D) will need an adapter.

title: How do I find English-speaking doctors in Belgium?
snippet: Many doctors in Brussels and other major cities speak English, especially in international clinics. See the Healthcare section for guidance on finding one.
keywords: How do I find English-speaking doctors in Belgium?
Many doctors in Brussels and other major cities speak English, especially in international clinics. See the Healthcare section for guidance on finding one.

snippet: VFS Global centres in India continue to process Belgium short-stay visa applications; standard processing remains around 15 days for complete applications.

snippet: The Airport Express train continues to be the quickest way from Brussels Airport into the city centre, taking around 18 minutes to Brussels-Central.

title: Health Insurance
snippet: Belgium has mandatory health insurance for residents through a 'mutualité'/'ziekenfonds'. Visitors should carry travel medical insurance covering at least €30,000.

keywords: Registering with a local GP gives you a consistent point of contact and referrals to specialists.

snippet: Belgium has a high standard of hospital care, both public and private, in all major cities.

keywords: Indian students admitted to a Belgian institution need a long-stay Type D student visa, applied for through VFS Global after receiving an admission letter.

title: Student Discounts
snippet: Student cards (often the university ID) unlock discounts on transport, museums and more.

title: Libraries

title: Work Permit / Single Permit
snippet: Non-EU nationals generally need a 'single permit' combining work and residence authorisation, usually applied for by the employer.
keywords: Non-EU nationals generally need a 'single permit' combining work and residence authorisation, usually applied for by the employer.

title: Taxes
snippet: Belgium has a progressive personal income tax system; residents file an annual tax return.
keywords: Belgium has a progressive personal income tax system; residents file an annual tax return.

title: Banking
snippet: Opening a Belgian bank account is usually needed for salary payment and daily life.
keywords: Opening a Belgian bank account is usually needed for salary payment and daily life.

title: Salary
snippet: Salary levels vary significantly by sector and city; Brussels and Antwerp generally offer higher wages than smaller cities.
keywords: Salary levels vary significantly by sector and city; Brussels and Antwerp generally offer higher wages than smaller cities.

title: Social Security
snippet: Employees and employers contribute to Belgium's social security system, covering healthcare, pensions and unemployment benefits.
keywords: Employees and employers contribute to Belgium's social security system, covering healthcare, pensions and unemployment benefits.

title: Emergency Numbers
snippet: 112 for general emergencies, 101 for police, 100 for ambulance/fire. See the Emergency Contacts section for the full list.

title: Lost Passport
snippet: Report a lost or stolen passport to the local police first to get an official report, then contact the Embassy of India in Brussels for an emergency certificate or replacement.
keywords: Report a lost or stolen passport to the local police first to get an official report, then contact the Embassy of India in Brussels for an emergency certificate or replacement.

title: Lost Wallet
snippet: Act quickly to block bank cards and report the loss.
keywords: Act quickly to block bank cards and report the loss.

title: Scam Awareness
snippet: Belgium is generally safe, but be alert to common tourist-area scams.
keywords: Belgium is generally safe, but be alert to common tourist-area scams.

title: Passport
snippet: Must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from the Schengen area, issued within the last 10 years, with at least 2 blank pages.
keywords: Must be valid for at least 3 months beyond your planned departure from the Schengen area, issued within the last 10 years, with at least 2 blank pages.

title: OCI (Overseas Citizen of India)
snippet: Persons of Indian origin who have taken foreign citizenship can apply for an OCI card, which functions as a lifelong multi-entry visa to India.
keywords: Persons of Indian origin who have taken foreign citizenship can apply for an OCI card, which functions as a lifelong multi-entry visa to India.

title: Schengen Travel Insurance

keywords: Mandatory for the visa application — must cover at least €30,000 for medical emergencies and repatriation, valid across the whole Schengen area for your full trip duration.

title: Long-Stay / Resident Insurance

keywords: Once resident, you'll typically move to mandatory Belgian health insurance (mutualité/ziekenfonds) rather than travel insurance.

title: Currency Exchange

keywords: Belgium uses the Euro (EUR). Exchange bureaus are available at the airport and city centres, though rates are often better via ATM withdrawal.

title: ATMs & Cards

keywords: Contactless debit/credit cards (Visa, Mastercard) are widely accepted across Belgium, including on public transport.

title: Municipality Registration

title: Brussels

keywords: Belgium's capital and the de facto capital of the EU — home to the Grand Place, EU institutions, and the largest Indian community in Belgium.

title: Antwerp

keywords: Belgium's largest port city, a global diamond trading hub with a significant Indian (especially Gujarati/Jain) business community.

title: Ghent
snippet: A medieval university city with canals, a lively student population and striking Gothic architecture.
keywords: A medieval university city with canals, a lively student population and striking Gothic architecture.

title: Bruges
snippet: A UNESCO World Heritage old town famed for its canals, chocolate shops and medieval charm.
keywords: A UNESCO World Heritage old town famed for its canals, chocolate shops and medieval charm.

title: Leuven

keywords: Home to KU Leuven, one of Europe's oldest universities, and a large international student population.

title: Liège

keywords: A historic industrial city on the Meuse river with a French-speaking university community.

title: Namur

keywords: Capital of Wallonia, known for its citadel overlooking the Meuse and Sambre rivers.

title: Mechelen
